page: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, supported by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and network connectivity is established at a baseline speed of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached operations and uncached large-block reads and writes placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also demonstrate top-tier capabilities. However, while LLM prompt processing speeds are in the top tier across small, medium, and large models, LLM text generation speeds range from average to weak, showing a performance tradeoff in sequential token generation.

Given its high resource density and dedicated physical cores, the m7a.48xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale enterprise workloads that require massive parallel processing without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation pipelines, static web serving at scale, and parallel compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong single-core capabilities. However, a notable tradeoff exists between its top-tier LLM prompt processing and its average-to-weak LLM text generation speeds. This instance is highly suited for large-scale parallel processing, software compilation, high-throughput database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge features 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated core instance delivering top-tier multi-core compute and high-speed DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores at 3.7 GHz.
Equipped with 768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
Well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
